spec_match.rationale: Models the spec distinction that only a non-empty trigger_context_id lifts the per-source aggregatable report cap. Chrome DevTools MCP verified non-empty, empty-string, and omitted-field modes against the backend route: non-empty accepted 42/42 while empty and omitted accepted 20/42 and dropped 22.

interactive.rationale: Range input plus radio modes for non-empty string, empty string, and omitted trigger_context_id update the pre/post comparison, backend status, and emitted header preview in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises a trigger-registration probe comparing eligibility. It now shows the real backend response header, accepted/dropped counts, and the cap behavior for each trigger_context_id state.

edge_cases.rationale: The previous hidden fallback and empty-string gaps are covered. Backend failures are surfaced as a visible fallback state, and empty string remains capped rather than being treated like a valid non-empty context ID.

references.rationale: Includes the ChromeStatus feature link and Attribution Reporting API spec link.

design_system.rationale: Uses design-system color variables and wraps the backend header preview at 390px mobile emulation without document-level horizontal overflow.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 through chrome-devtools-mcp. Exercised the conversion slider and the non-empty, empty string, and omitted trigger_context_id radio states; all route requests returned expected counts with no app JS errors. The only console error was the expected localhost Attribution Reporting attestation warning.
